context: PR #42 is open on the current branch with no human review comments and no AI-reviewer bot installed. The diff has a couple of latent issues the agent's own review can catch (one reproducible bug, one nit), and fixing the bug touches code that warrants a second look. The project has a test harness and passing CI.
expected_behavior: Runs an iterative loop (default max 5) driven by peep's OWN discovery review (Step 3: subagent-per-file + validator, or 3-pass vote) — it does NOT require or wait on an external AI-reviewer bot

Each iteration: review the diff → fix actionable findings under the reproduction-first evidence bar (Step 4 / Evidence) → resolve → validate (Step 10) → commit 'fix: self-review pass (peep loop iteration N)' → push → re-review the updated diff
On iterations >= 2 it focuses on what changed since the last push (plus anything still unresolved), so it converges rather than re-litigating settled code
The reproducible bug is fixed (reproduced/tooling-confirmed) and the nit applied; anything it cannot reproduce is declined with a rationale, not papered over to look clean
Terminates when a review pass yields no new actionable findings (clean) OR the iteration cap OR no-progress (zero new fixes in an iteration), and summarizes the result
If an AI-reviewer bot WERE installed, it would optionally fold the bot's findings in — but the loop works fully without one
failure_modes: Treats --loop as requiring an external bot (posts '@greptile review' / waits on a bot) when none is installed, instead of running the agent's own review
Single-pass: reviews and fixes once but never re-reviews the updated diff
Loops forever / ignores the max-iterations cap and the no-progress guard
Applies a cosmetic 'fix' to make a finding disappear (appeasement) instead of declining an unreproducible one
Re-litigates the whole diff every iteration (no convergence / no dedupe), or pushes code that fails validation to keep looping
